tools.name: test_payram_connection
tools.description: Checks the /api/v1/payment endpoint on a Payram server using baseUrl and apiKey. If they are not provided, returns a .env template you can add to your workspace.

tools.name: payram_doctor
tools.description: Staged, read-only health diagnosis: server reachability -> merchant API key -> admin JWT -> payment readiness (wallets + listener workers). Returns ranked likely causes with exact fix commands for the first failing stage. Run this FIRST when anything PayRam-related fails.

tools.name: generate_payment_sdk_snippet
tools.description: Generates backend code using the official Payram JS/TS SDK to create a payment.

tools.name: generate_payment_http_snippet
tools.description: Generates a raw HTTP sample for creating a Payram payment in the requested language.

tools.name: generate_payment_status_snippet
tools.description: Generates backend code to query the status of a Payram payment.

tools.name: generate_payment_route_snippet
tools.description: Generates a ready-to-use backend endpoint (e.g., /api/pay/create) that creates a Payram payment.

tools.name: snippet_nextjs_payment_route
tools.description: Returns a Next.js App Router API route that calls Payram's create-payment HTTP API.

tools.name: snippet_express_payment_route
tools.description: Returns an Express router that posts to Payram's /api/v1/payment endpoint.

tools.name: snippet_fastapi_payment_route
tools.description: Returns a FastAPI handler that calls Payram's create-payment HTTP API.

tools.name: snippet_laravel_payment_route
tools.description: Returns a Laravel controller that posts to Payram's /api/v1/payment endpoint.

tools.name: snippet_go_payment_handler
tools.description: Returns a Gin handler that proxies /api/pay/create to Payram's create-payment API.

tools.name: snippet_spring_payment_controller
tools.description: Returns a Spring Boot controller that calls Payram's /api/v1/payment endpoint.

tools.name: generate_payout_sdk_snippet
tools.description: Generates a backend code snippet for creating a payout using the Payram JS/TS SDK.

tools.name: generate_payout_status_snippet
tools.description: Generates backend code to query the status of a payout using the Payram SDK.

tools.name: generate_payout_recipient_flow_snippet
tools.description: Generates the recommended OTP-verified payout flow: create recipient → validate OTP → create payout against the saved recipient. Use this for repeat beneficiaries; use generate_payout_sdk_snippet for one-off direct payouts.

tools.name: generate_referral_sdk_snippet
tools.description: Generates a backend route or service snippet to create a referral event using the Payram SDK.

tools.name: generate_referral_validation_snippet
tools.description: Generates a snippet to validate referral IDs, statuses, and eligibility.

tools.name: generate_referral_status_snippet
tools.description: Generates code to fetch referral progress, rewards, or status.

tools.name: generate_referral_route_snippet
tools.description: Generates a backend route such as /api/referrals/create for logging referral events.

tools.name: generate_webhook_handler
tools.description: Generates backend code to handle Payram webhook HTTP requests.

tools.name: generate_webhook_event_router
tools.description: Generates a backend event router that dispatches Payram webhook events to domain handlers.

tools.name: generate_mock_webhook_event
tools.description: Generates a snippet to send mock Payram webhook events to your local endpoint for testing.

tools.name: assess_payram_project
tools.description: Inspects dependency files, frameworks, and .env status to suggest the next integration actions.

tools.name: explain_payram_basics
tools.description: Explain Payram's product pillars, architecture, payments, and payouts capabilities.

tools.name: get_payram_links
tools.description: Surface official documentation, website, and community links.

tools.name: explain_payment_flow
tools.description: Describe how payments move from customer initiation through settlement.

tools.name: explain_payram_concepts
tools.description: Glossary-backed explanation of Payram terminology and constraints.

tools.name: explain_referrals_basics
tools.description: Summarize how Payram referral campaigns are configured and managed.

tools.name: explain_referral_flow
tools.description: Detail the referrer/referee lifecycle and required APIs.

tools.name: get_referral_dashboard_guide
tools.description: Explain how to embed and manage the referral dashboard experience.

tools.name: prepare_payram_test
tools.description: Confirm hosting, credentials, and environment variables before generating demo apps.

tools.name: get_payram_doc_by_id
tools.description: Returns the markdown for a Payram doc given its id, e.g. "features/payouts".

tools.name: list_payram_docs
tools.description: Lists the available Payram doc ids relative to docs/payram-docs-live. Optionally scope by a prefix such as "features".

tools.name: generate_env_template
tools.description: Creates a .env template for configuring a merchant backend to talk to a self-hosted Payram server.

tools.name: generate_setup_checklist
tools.description: Returns a step-by-step checklist of everything a merchant must configure to start using Payram.

tools.name: suggest_file_structure
tools.description: Suggests a recommended backend folder/file structure for integrating Payram.

tools.name: get_agent_setup_flow
tools.description: Returns the step-by-step setup flow for deploying PayRam as an agent. Covers install, wallet creation, faucet funding, contract deployment, and first payment. Includes chain recommendations (ETH Sepolia for testnet, Base for mainnet), faucet URLs, card-to-crypto prerequisites, and status/recovery co

tools.name: onboard_agent_setup
tools.description: Returns the complete autonomous agent setup guide for deploying Payram without any web UI or human interaction.

tools.name: scaffold_payram_app
tools.description: Generates a minimal full-stack app skeleton with Payram payments and payouts routes (and a simple UI) for a chosen language and framework.

tools.name: list_platforms
tools.description: Lists all external platforms (projects) for the authenticated user. Use this to discover your platform ID, which other tools auto-resolve if omitted.

tools.name: get_payment_summary
tools.description: Returns payment counts: total, open, closed, and cancelled. Useful for a quick overview of payment activity.

tools.name: lookup_payment
tools.description: Look up payments by transaction hash, email, reference ID, customer ID, or invoice ID. Returns up to 5 matching payments with full details.

tools.name: search_payments
tools.description: Search and filter payments with full control over status, network, currency, dates, webhook status, and pagination. Returns a paginated list of matching payments.

tools.name: get_unswept_balances
tools.description: Returns unswept (unsettled) balances across all wallets, broken down by blockchain and currency. Shows sweep readiness status for each entry (sweep, sweep_in_progress, sweep_not_allowed, no_balance).

tools.name: get_daily_volume
tools.description: Returns the total payment volume for a given date, with breakdowns by network and currency. Only counts FILLED (completed) payments. Defaults to today if no date is specified.

tools.name: list_currencies
tools.description: Lists every blockchain currency PayRam supports on this node (chain code, network, currency code). Public endpoint — works with only PAYRAM_BASE_URL set, no API key or JWT required. Use this to discover valid blockchainCode/currencyCode values before creating payments or payouts.

tools.name: list_recipients
tools.description: Lists saved withdrawal recipients (payout beneficiaries). Use this to find a recipient ID for the 3-step payout flow, or to check whether a beneficiary is already OTP-verified (status "active") before creating a payout.

tools.name: create_payment_link
tools.description: Creates a PayRam checkout (payment) link and returns the hosted URL plus a reference_id. Authenticates with the Merchant API-Key (set PAYRAM_API_KEY). This WRITES — it creates a real payment request. The customer chooses the chain/currency on the hosted checkout. Poll status later with lookup_paymen

tools.name: check_node_sync
tools.description: Per-chain node health verdict: healthy / lagging / unreachable / listener-down. Computes how old each RPC node’s last block is — any non-BTC chain older than 10 minutes (BTC: 90 minutes, since BTC blocks every ~10m) is flagged as lagging or not syncing. Also checks the chain’s listener worker. When

tools.name: restart_payram_worker
tools.description: REMEDIATION ACTION (write): restarts a blockchain listener worker via supervisor — the minimal fix when check_node_sync reports a chain as lagging or listener-down. Workflow: run check_node_sync first → restart the named worker → wait ~60s → run check_node_sync again to confirm recovery. A restart d

tools.name: check_payment_readiness
tools.description: Diagnoses what is NOT yet set up to accept payments, per blockchain: missing deposit wallet, chain/currency not enabled for the project, or a stopped listener worker. Read-only — reports gaps and points to the CLI for wallet setup; it does not create wallets itself.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "test_payram_connection" references sensitive artifacts (.env, apikey). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Confirm hosting, credentials, and environment variables before generating demo apps.

findings.detail: Description of "generate_env_template" references sensitive artifacts (.env). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Creates a .env template for configuring a merchant backend to talk to a self-hosted Payram server.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "create_payment_link" references sensitive artifacts (api_key). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Creates a PayRam checkout (payment) link and returns the hosted URL plus a reference_id. Authenticates with the Merchant API-Key (set PAYRAM_API_KEY). This WRIT…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp.payram.com" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_agent_setup_flow, create_payment_link · Private data: test_payram_connection, payram_doctor, assess_payram_project, prepare_payram_test, suggest_file_structure, get_agent_setup_flow, list_currencies, check_payment_readiness · Public sink: generate_webhook_handler, generate_webhook_event_router, generate_mock_webhook_event, lookup_payment, search_payments · Destructive: payram_doctor, check_node_sync, restart_payram_worker.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_agent_setup_flow, create_payment_link · Private data: test_payram_connection, payram_doctor, assess_payram_project, prepare_payram_test, suggest_file_structure, get_agent_setup_flow, list_currencies, check_payment_readiness · Public sink: generate_webhook_handler, generate_webhook_event_router, generate_mock_webhook_event, lookup_payment, search_payments · Destructive: payram_doctor, check_node_sync, restart_payram_worker

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
